Other: 2030: At 8:30 PM, a brilliant meteor crossed the sky to the Wd. Exploding a short distance from the ship with an audible report & lighted up the entire sky.

Note: Noon: His long mght be 26 not 28 degrees.
0505: Cape Good Hope Lt brg ENE. 0545 cc NXE.
0800: Depart. Robben Id Lt in line with E end of Table Mtn brg S18W and Duyker Pt S39W. E end Robben Id S6W.
0645: S end St Helena Id brg S78E; N end N84E.
0800: E Pt St. Helena brg S28E; W Pt S44W.

1800: Changed Baro from 29.98 to 30.08. Too big a jump down then up for no apparent reason. The mercurial barometer is subject to rapid variations in height, called pumping, due to pitch and roll of the vessel and temporary changes in atmospheric pressure in the vicinity of the barometer. Because of this ... the mercurial barometer has been replaced at sea by the aneroid barometer. https://www.btb.termiumplus.gc.ca/tpv2alpha/alpha-eng.html?lang=eng&i=&index=ent&srchtxt=BAROMETER%20PUMPING
Noon: Baro all over the place. Ship rolling deeply and suddenly thru much of the day.
8AM: Once again, the pressures are all over the place.

Other: 0900: Between 9 and 9:30 the colors of the water changed from a dark green to a deep blue and the temperature from 67 to 75.

Note: 1100: cc N1/2W. 1100: Land on port bow about 4 mi dist.
2002: Anchored. Off Boston Light. Brg SxE dist about 1/4 mi.
Noon: He reports rain and snow in the Event area. Prob of snow with temp 45 wet bulb 45 is 0. Noon: Boston Navy Yard
